summary: Iteration 33 (Staff Reflective Practice & Wellbeing Hub — 'My Reflection') FULL GREEN. All 14 backend tests in test_iteration33.py pass in 30.4s. Extra cross-user privacy probe confirmed: staff GET on manager's private entry returns 403. Frontend Playwright covered: staff avatar-dropdown navigation, /reflection page render, quick check-in flow, shift_reflection (7 prompts), win (private), guided/Gibbs (6 prompts), pattern nudge after 3 stressed/overwhelmed check-ins with supportive copy ('You've been stretched lately'), privacy regression (staff redirected from /reflection/supervision/* to /), manager flow (sidebar still 7 items, NO 'My Reflection' shortcut, dropdown link works, Team Wellbeing tab renders TeamWellbeingAwarenessCard, named amber row navigates to supervision view, private 'Helped Maddy' win NOT leaked, shared 'Tired but proud' entry IS visible), senior role (has dropdown link but redirected from supervision route, Team Wellbeing tab hidden), mobile 390x844 renders cleanly. Zero console errors throughout. Iter28-31 regression PASS; 2 pre-existing flaky iter32 failures (seed-drift fall count, transient PDF latency 30s) are unrelated to iter33.

minor.issue: Transient 30s latency on one PDF run vs <5s threshold — preview cold-start; PASSED on iter33 re-run window, not caused by reflection feature.
Maggie seed drifted to 6 fall events (test bound 2-5) — seed-data drift from accumulated test runs, not iter33.

issues: CheckInModal renders as a centered full-viewport overlay rather than a true bottom-sheet as described in spec. Functionally fine and mobile-readable, but if a true bottom-sheet (slide-up, partial height with handle) is intended, the modal CSS would need transform-origin / bottom anchoring. LOW priority cosmetic.

privacy_verification.staff_cannot_read_other_users_private_en: PASS — 403 returned (covered by test_iteration33::test_staff_cannot_read_others_shared_reflections AND extra adhoc probe in /tmp/staff_b_check.py)
privacy_verification.manager_supervision_view_only_shared: PASS — private 'Helped Maddy' win NOT present; shared 'Tired but proud' shift reflection IS present
privacy_verification.team_awareness_named_only_when_shared: PASS — Alex Staff named because they shared a reflection earlier; would be anonymised otherwise
privacy_verification.pattern_nudge_copy: Supportive language confirmed: 'You've been stretched lately', 'That's a lot to carry', 'Would booking a chat with your manager — or just taking 10 minutes for yourself — help today?', 'Only you see this nudge'. NON-PUNITIVE.

verified_behaviour: Backend: 14/14 reflection tests pass (prompt_sets endpoint, checkin CRUD own-only, reflection private-by-default, share toggle, owner-only edit/delete, staff cannot read other private, supportive pattern nudge after 3 stress, supervision shows only shared, supervision manager-only, supervision flag on stress pattern, team-awareness named-only-when-shared + manager-only, 401 on unauth).
Staff avatar dropdown: user-card opens menu with user-menu-reflection + logout-btn. Sidebar has NO 'My Reflection' (compliant with locked 7-item rule).
Reflection page: 'A space that's just yours' header, 4 tabs (tab-home/tab-shift/tab-wins/tab-checkins), open-checkin-btn + open-reflection-btn present.
CheckIn flow: modal -> mood (stressed) -> context (after_shift) -> note -> submit -> toast 'Reflection saved/check-in saved' -> modal closes -> appears in Check-ins tab.

Shift reflection: default kind=shift_reflection, all 7 prompts present (feel, went_well, challenging, emotional, supported, manager_aware, proud), share toggle works, entry saved + appears on Shift reflections tab with 'Shared with manager'.
Win: kind switch to win, title + body, private by default — appears with 'PRIVATE' lock badge.
Guided/Gibbs: kind switch to guided, prompt-set <select> changes to gibbs, all 6 Gibbs prompts render (description, feelings, evaluation, analysis, conclusion, action_plan), entry saved.
Pattern nudge: red-tinted card renders on /reflection after 3 stressed/overwhelmed check-ins in 14 days; supportive copy.
Manager: 7-item sidebar (Dashboard, Children's, Adult, Home Ops, Staff Ops, Compliance, Admin) — NO 'My Reflection'. Avatar dropdown still has 'My Reflection'.

Manager Team Wellbeing tab: team-awareness-card shows amber count '1 team member may benefit', named row 'Alex Staff · 3 stretched check-ins (shared)'. Click navigates to /reflection/supervision/<id>.

Supervision view: shows staff name 'Alex Staff', mood mix bar chart (Stressed 67%, Overwhelmed 33%), pattern note '3 stretched-feeling check-ins in 14 days', supervision flag card 'Wellbeing check-in recommended', Shared reflections (1) block with the shared shift reflection only. Private win NOT shown.
Senior role: has My Reflection in dropdown; redirected from supervision route; cannot see Team Wellbeing tab.
Mobile 390x844: page renders, header + buttons stack, tabs wrap, pattern nudge legible, check-in modal fills viewport and remains operable. No layout overflow.
action_items: (OPTIONAL/LOW) If a true bottom-sheet UX is intended for CheckInModal on mobile, anchor it to the viewport bottom with slide-up animation; currently it is a centered full-viewport overlay (functionally OK).

(NOT BLOCKING — pre-existing) iter32 has 2 brittle assertions (Maggie fall count bound 2-5 now seeing 6 due to cumulative test runs, and a PDF 5s threshold that flaked at 30s on cold start) — consider widening bounds OR fixture-resetting seed data.
critical_code_review_comments: Privacy-first architecture is solid: backend enforces owner-only reads on private entries, manager supervision endpoint filters shared_with_manager=true, team awareness aggregates anonymised counts and only names when explicit share exists. Pattern detection uses 14-day window with non-punitive copy on both sides (staff nudge + manager 'recommended' framing).

Sidebar discipline maintained: still 6 (staff) / 7 (manager) locked items; 'My Reflection' correctly lives only in avatar dropdown, and the manager-side surface is a tab inside Staff Operations rather than a new sidebar entry.

Hybrid privacy UX is well-communicated to users: page subtitle, supervision-view banner, and pattern-nudge footer all state 'manager only sees what you share' / 'aggregate signal — no detail' — strong non-surveillance framing.

Reflection.jsx is 893 lines — borderline over the 700-line guideline; consider extracting CheckInModal, ReflectionEditor, and entry renderers into /components/reflection/ for maintainability. Not blocking.
Prompt set <select> uses native HTML select rather than shadcn Select — minor consistency issue but works fine across browsers and screen-reader friendly.

success_rate.backend: 100% (14/14 iter33; 61/63 iter28-32 regression — 2 pre-existing flakes unrelated)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all spec'd flows verified, zero console errors

seed_data_creation: Created during run (will linger): 3 check-ins for Alex Staff (1 stressed + 1 overwhelmed + 1 stressed), 1 shared shift_reflection ('Tired but proud' / 'Helped 3 residents calmly'), 1 private win ('Helped Maddy' / 'She opened up.'), 1 guided/Gibbs entry ('A difficult moment...'). One TEST_MGRPRIV manager-private entry created and deleted in adhoc privacy probe.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter33 backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration33.py (14 tests, all green ~30s). Frontend testids verified: user-card, user-menu, user-menu-reflection, logout-btn, reflection-page, open-checkin-btn, open-reflection-btn, tab-home/shift/wins/checkins (NOT tab-overview/shift_reflection/win/checkin as spec said — actual ids are tab-home/tab-shift/tab-wins/tab-checkins), checkin-modal, checkin-mood-*, checkin-context-*, checkin-note, checkin-submit, reflection-editor, reflection-kind-*, reflection-prompt-set, reflection-prompt-*, reflection-title, reflection-body, reflection-share-toggle, reflection-save, pattern-nudge, staffops-tab-wellbeing, team-awareness-card, amber-{userId}, supervision-view, shared-reflections-block. Pre-existing iter32 flakes do not need iter33 retest. Main agent may want to update the review spec doc to reflect the actual tab ids.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature complete and privacy-first behaviour verified end-to-end.
